---
title: "Low-power wide-area networks and IoT connectivity"
chapter-id: S10
status: outline
---

::: {.callout-note title="Chapter outline"}
The scope and dependency boundary are stable. Full exposition, worked examples,
exercises, and tested practical evidence still need authoring and review.
:::

## Central question

How do you move small messages reliably over long distances and years of battery
life within legal and duty-cycle limits?

## Learning outcomes

After completing this chapter, the reader should be able to:

- choose a link technology from range, throughput, energy-per-message, and
  regulatory limits;
- compute a link budget and duty-cycle-limited throughput for a candidate
  deployment;
- design an application message and retry scheme that survives loss without
  draining energy;
- secure device identity, keys, and updates across an intermittent link.

## Concept path

- LPWAN families (LoRa and LoRaWAN, cellular IoT, and alternatives);
- link budgets, spreading, and duty-cycle regulation;
- energy-per-message and battery-life estimation;
- application protocols and store-and-forward delivery;
- provisioning, identity, keys, and secure update over constrained links;
- coexistence, scaling, and gateway or backhaul dependence.

## Evidence and practice

The completed chapter will include a prediction, a worked model, a simulation or
executable check where it adds value, measured or authentic data, a fault or
model mismatch, and a requirements-based design decision. The final exercise set
will mix retrieval, calculation, interpretation, debugging, and open design.

## Connections

Chapter ID: `S10`. Prerequisites and downstream course mappings are maintained in
[`chapters.toml`](../chapters.toml). The [reading roadmap](../roadmap.qmd)
provides reader-facing routes and the practice spine.
